summary.intent: Address a batch of review comments from Codex on PR #665 in fogodev/ars-ui, covering selection keyboard behavior (Shift+Arrow range selection), id sync on prop changes, item activation guarding, select content active-descendant, trigger open-state arrow/commit behavior, and hidden required input handling.
summary.outcome: All targeted fixes were implemented, local tests (unit, proptest, clippy, fmt, i18n feature check) passed, snapshots were updated and accepted, and the commit was pushed to the PR branch with the snapshot-reviewed label re-applied.

repo: Selection logic is split across ars-collections (core state machine in selection.rs) and ars-components (listbox and select UI components in crates/ars-components/src/selection/).
ComponentIds is defined in ars-core (crates/ars-core/src/component_ids.rs), not inline in the component files.
Snapshot tests live alongside the module under crates/ars-components/src/selection/select/snapshots/ and require `cargo insta accept` after behavioral changes.
The PR uses a `snapshot-reviewed` label that must be re-applied after any snapshot change via `gh pr edit --add-label snapshot-reviewed`.
Codex bot reviews are tracked via GitHub reactions (👀) on issue #665 and GraphQL unresolved review threads; polling both is necessary to detect new feedback.
Proptest cases for state machines live under --test proptest_state_machines and require --ignored flag to run; PROPTEST_CASES=256 is the standard run count.

code.finding: Shift+Arrow range/extend selection logic and item activation guarding were patched here; active-descendant attribute wiring also lives in this range.
Additional listbox keyboard handling (anchor/range state) patched in this section.
Trigger open-state arrow and commit behavior, hidden required input handling, and id sync on prop changes were patched across this region.
Select content active-descendant attribute patched here; this change produced a new snapshot (ars_components__selection__select__tests__select_content_multi.snap).
Core range/extend/anchor selection state machine lives here; referenced during investigation but not directly modified in this session.

workflow: CI gates run in approximate order: clippy → workspace check → formatting → snapshot → mutual-exclusion → unit tests → longer jobs; fast gates finish well before unit tests.
Polling Codex feedback requires waiting for it to re-review after each push; 👀 reaction persists until Codex submits a new review, making it an unreliable real-time signal.
Using `cargo insta accept` after a behavioral change is required before re-running tests to avoid false failures from stale snapshots.
Combining fmt, test, clippy, feature check, and git diff --check into a single pre-commit verification sequence is the established pattern for this repo.
Codex review comments can contain duplicates; filtering to the unique actionable set before patching avoids redundant work.

friction: Codex bot was slow to move off 👀 and re-review after pushes, requiring multiple long sleep-poll cycles (120s, 240s, 300s) before new feedback appeared.
Codex's review batch contained duplicate comments, requiring manual deduplication before patching.
A formatting-only fix was needed before the main patch because a prior commit introduced formatting drift, triggering an extra Codex re-trigger cycle.
Unit Tests CI job was significantly slower than the fast gates, extending the feedback loop before the full green signal was available.
open_items: Codex may post additional review rounds on the latest commit; the session ended while Codex was still in 👀 state — further feedback may be pending.
Longer CI jobs beyond unit tests were still running at the end of the session; their results were not confirmed.
The duplicate-comment pattern in Codex reviews suggests its prompt or diffing logic may need tuning upstream.
